title: zannichellia palustris subvar. gibberosa
intro: Zannichellia palustris subvar. gibberosa, commonly known as the stonewort, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Potamogetonaceae. The stonewort has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of stonewort include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to act as an antihistamine and antiseptic. Stonewort can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in herbal teas and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
